text:
A selection of Aussie athletes gunning to take part in the Paris Olympics this year have showcased the Australian kit for the first time.

The Australian Olympic team has celebrated one of the most special occasions of the lead-up to an Olympics campaign, the unveiling of the official uniform.

Eleven Olympians and Paris 2024 hopefuls represented the new kit at the iconic Yurong Point at the Botanic Gardens in Sydney including gold-medal winning rower in Tokyo Alex Purnell, legendary Matildas goalkeeper Lydia Williams, Olympic bronze medalist in Javelin Kelsey Lee-Barber, and longest-serving Australian hockey player Eddie Ockenden.

Brandon Starc (High Jump), Maurice Longbottom (Rugby 7s), Charlotte Caslick (Rugby 7s), Eileen Cikamatana (Weightlifting), Grae Morris (Sailing), Izzi Batt-Doyle (Athletics), and Mariah Williams (Hockey) were also part of the squad on show.

The new uniforms were manufactured by ASICS and each feature green and gold colour schemes as well as an Indigenous print, making it the largest integration of Indigenous prints on athletes' uniforms in the history of the summer Olympics.

Sports minister Anika Wells heaped praise on those involved with the design and for reaching this achievement, while speaking at the kit launch on Thursday.

“Thank you to our Indigenous artist and Olympic boxer Paul Fleming and to Torres Strait artist David Bosun for your designs and for sharing 60,000 years of your culture,” she said.

“Days like this are important because it is the fanfare that our Olympians deserve.

“Many of our greatest Olympians have to work part time while competing against the world’s best, where every four years their ability is decided by an inch or a millisecond – and it’s their fortitude that I want to pay credit to this morning.”

Each item of uniform has been specifically tailored to suit the motion of each sport with about 460 to 480 athletes set to make up the Australian team.

Chef de Mission of the Australian Olympic Team for the upcoming games and former gold medallist Olympian Anna Meares said she “cannot wait” to witness more iconic moments in Australian sporting history occur in the new uniforms.

“So many iconic Australian sporting moments played out in ASICS kit at the Tokyo Olympics, and I cannot wait to see the new sporting history Australian athletes will create in Paris in these uniforms,” she said in a statement.

“We are proud to work with ASICS and the artists to feature Aboriginal and Torres Strait artwork throughout the uniform, bringing the stories of these designs to a global audience of billions.

While many Aussies have already qualified for the Games, some are still yet to secure their place with more qualification events set for the coming months.

title: Members of the Australian Olympic team officially unveil uniforms for Paris Games featuring Indigenous design | Sky News Australia
